name: Lake Shore Railroad Yard, South End of Sandusky
description: Three sidings of iron rail converging on the main depot, crowded with freight cars waiting for the morning consist and lit by a pair of yard lanterns that cast more shadow than light. The night freight from Cleveland is expected in forty minutes, and the yard foreman is somewhere among the cars with a lantern.
atmosphericDetails: The smell of the yard is coal smoke, machine oil, and the particular iron smell of the rails themselves — a smell that Thomas Cole has known since childhood when his father brought him here.
A deadheading railroad man is asleep in the waycar of the nearest consist, his lantern turned low; he has seen nothing or everything, it is impossible to tell which.

The telegraph line enters the depot building through a conduit in the east wall, and the junction box where the Norwalk spur connects to the main line is accessible from the yard — this is where a phantomed circuit could be introduced.
The tender of a stationary engine on the middle siding is half-full of coal, and the firebox is cold — this engine is not going anywhere tonight, which means the siding is clear.
